decisions.briefing: Markets slipped modestly today with the S&P 500 down 0.7% and NASDAQ off 0.9%, reflecting a mild risk-off tone. Cash is at $298.25 — below the $500 minimum required to open new positions — so no new buys are possible even though 3 slots are open and MRNA, MDGL, and APGE all score 100. Holding all 7 current positions and letting automated stop-losses and profit targets manage downside risk.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities pulled back on June 3, 2026, with the NASDAQ declining 0.9% and the S&P 500 losing 0.7%, as broad-based selling pressure weighed on technology and large-cap names. The move appeared to reflect macro caution and modest profit-taking rather than any single catalyst, with no sector providing meaningful support. Markets settled in neutral-to-negative territory as investors remained reluctant to add risk into the session close.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年6月3日，美股普遍回落，那斯達克指數下跌0.9%，標普500指數下跌0.7%，科技股及大型股均承壓。此次跌勢似乎源於宏觀情緒謹慎及近期升幅後的獲利回吐，而非單一利空消息所致，各板塊缺乏明顯支撐。市場在欠缺新催化劑的情況下，收盤維持中性偏弱格局。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag is the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a portfolio — cash earns little while markets potentially rise. Today's portfolio illustrates the flip side: with only $298.25 in cash (below the $500 buy threshold), there is not enough dry powder to act on three high-scoring opportunities even though position slots are available. Maintaining a sufficient cash buffer is a balancing act — too much drags returns, too little removes tactical flexibility when good setups appear.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指組合持有未投資現金所產生的機會成本——現金幾乎不產生回報，而市場卻可能持續上漲。今日組合正好體現了另一面：現金僅剩298.25美元，低於500美元的建倉門檻，即使有三個空位，也無法把握MRNA等高分機會。維持適當現金儲備需要拿捏平衡——現金太多拖累表現，太少則在好機會出現時失去靈活部署的能力。
